doc.title: Pension Expense & Funding
doc.event: Vaughan Corp. sponsors a defined benefit pension plan. For the current year, the actuary reports service cost of $85,000 and a 7% settlement rate on a beginning PBO of $600,000. Expected and actual return on plan assets was $30,000. At year-end Vaughan funds the plan; the contribution differs from pension expense. Prepare the entry to record pension expense and the funding contribution.

lines.why: Service cost 85,000 + interest cost 42,000 (7% × beginning PBO 600,000) − expected return 30,000.
lines.trap: Interest cost uses the settlement rate × BEGINNING PBO — not the rate on plan assets, not ending balances.

lines.why: The amount actually contributed to the plan trustee.
lines.trap: Cash = the contribution. It is NOT pension expense — funding and expensing are independent decisions.

lines.why: The plug. Expense exceeded funding by 2,000, so the net pension liability grows.
Same components: 85,000 + 42,000 − 30,000. Funding level never changes expense.
The plug flips to a debit — contributing 3,000 more than expense improves the funded position.
The amount contributed to the trustee.
lines.trap: Overfunding doesn't reduce pension expense — the extra 3,000 hits the balance-sheet account.

memorize.body: Pension expense = Service cost + Interest on beginning PBO − Expected return on plan assets + PSC amortization ± Gain/loss amortization
Interest cost = settlement rate × BEGINNING PBO. And it's the EXPECTED (not actual) return that reduces expense.
The credit to Cash is whatever was contributed. Pension Asset/Liability is the plug that balances the entry — it is never a choice.

questions.prompt: Service cost $85,000, interest cost $42,000, expected return $30,000, contribution $95,000. What is pension expense for the year?

distractors.feedback: You left out a component — expected return REDUCES expense: 85,000 + 42,000 − 30,000 = 97,000.
You flipped the return's direction. Expected return is SUBTRACTED — plan earnings make the pension cheaper, not pricier.
That's the contribution (cash), not pension expense. Funding and expensing are independent.

build.scaffold: Compute expense from components first (SC + IC − expected return). Credit cash for the contribution. Whatever's left is the Pension Asset/Liability plug.
